gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/344)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/344)
text: مَنْ خَرَجَ مِنْ مَنْزِلِهِ مُعْتَمّاً تَحْتَ حَنَكِهِ يُرِيدُ سَفَراً لَمْ يُصِبْهُ فِي سَفَرِهِ سَرَقٌ وَ لَا حَرَقٌ وَ لَا مَكْرُوهٌ .

en.hubeali: Abu Ali Al Ashary, from one of his companions, from Ali Bin Al Hakam,

(It has been narrated) raising it to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The one who goes out from his house wearing a turban (passing) beneath his chin, intending a journey, would neither be hit during his journey by theft, nor by burning, nor by abhorrence’.
en.sarwar: 6. It is narrated that: al-Tabiqiyah, a turban without tahannak (allowing one end to hang down on one’s front side and the other end down one’s backside), is the turban of Satan, may he be condemned.’”
